title: secure_zero word-aligned optimization may overflow on unaligned pointers
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_util.h:35-59 (secure_zero inline function)

Risk: The word-aligned optimization at lines 40-51 is safe, but:

1. Line 47: *(volatile unsigned long *)p = 0 - on architectures where unaligned word access traps (some ARM Cortex-M), if the alignment loop at lines 42-45 fails to fully align (e.g., len becomes 0 before alignment), subsequent iterations might cause alignment faults.

Wait - re-reading: the alignment loop decrements len each iteration, and the word loop only runs while len >= sizeof(unsigned long). So if we start with len=3 and need 4 bytes to align, the alignment loop consumes all of len before reaching the word loop.

Actually this is safe. The alignment loop will either:
a) Fully align the pointer before len becomes < sizeof(unsigned long)
b) Exhaust len during alignment, causing the word loop to be skipped

Let me re-examine for actual issues...
close_reason: Not a bug - alignment loop properly handles all cases by decrementing len before word loop checks
